name: llama.cpp
summary: Run LLaMA-family models on consumer hardware in pure C/C++.
description: llama.cpp is a port of Meta's LLaMA inference loop to plain C/C++ with no external dependencies. It pioneered the local-first LLM movement by making it possible to run multi-billion-parameter models on a single laptop through aggressive quantization and a tight CPU/GPU code path.

timeline.title: Initial public release
timeline.description: First commit enabling LLaMA inference on a MacBook.

timeline.title: GGUF format introduced
timeline.description: Successor to GGML becomes the default model packaging format.

timeline.title: Native multimodal support
timeline.description: Vision and audio backends land in the mainline.

name: Ollama
summary: One command to download, run, and manage open models locally.
description: Ollama packages llama.cpp behind a friendly CLI and a REST API. It maintains a model library (called a Modelfile) so that running a model locally is as simple as `ollama run llama3`.

timeline.title: First public release
timeline.description: Single-binary LLM runner with a curated Modelfile catalog.

timeline.title: OpenAI-compatible API
timeline.description: Local drop-in replacement for the OpenAI REST surface.

name: Transformers
summary: Reference library for state-of-the-art machine learning in PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX.
description: The Transformers library ships reference implementations of hundreds of open models, unified training pipelines, and the `pipeline()` API that lets you go from `text` to inference in a single line.

timeline.title: pytorch-pretrained-bert renamed to Transformers
timeline.description: BERT, GPT, GPT-2, and Transformer-XL share a single API.

timeline.title: Version 4.0
timeline.description: Switch to a single backend, on by default across PyTorch/TensorFlow/JAX.

timeline.title: Native quantization and KV-cache reuse
timeline.description: Bitsandbytes, GPTQ, and AWQ land in the mainline.

name: LangChain
summary: Framework for composing retrieval, tools, and chains on top of any model.
description: LangChain provides abstractions for prompts, retrievers, tool calling, and agent loops so you can compose production AI applications without rebuilding the same plumbing every time.

timeline.title: Initial open source release
timeline.description: Composable chains around OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.

timeline.title: LangChain v0.1
timeline.description: Stable package boundary between langchain-core and integrations.

name: Stable Diffusion WebUI
summary: Feature-rich browser interface for Stable Diffusion checkpoints.
description: AUTOMATIC1111's WebUI is the de-facto playground for Stable Diffusion: txt2img, img2img, inpainting, ControlNet, LoRA training, and dozens of extensions live behind a single Gradio app.

timeline.title: First public release
timeline.description: Gradio UI wrapping the original Stable Diffusion reference code.

timeline.title: ControlNet support
timeline.description: Plug-in conditioning models for pose, depth, and edges.

name: vLLM
summary: High-throughput LLM serving engine with PagedAttention.
description: vLLM introduces PagedAttention to eliminate KV-cache fragmentation, unlocking multi-x throughput for batched LLM serving. It is the default backend for production text generation behind many LLM gateways.

timeline.title: vLLM open source release
timeline.description: PagedAttention paper and reference implementation released.

name: Text Generation Inference
summary: Production-grade LLM serving stack from Hugging Face.
description: TGI is Hugging Face's Rust + Python serving runtime for LLMs. It batches requests, shards models across GPUs, and exposes an OpenAI-compatible HTTP API.

timeline.title: Initial release
timeline.description: Hugging Face's first production LLM serving stack.

name: ExLlamaV2
summary: Fast CUDA inference for quantized GPTQ and EXL2 models.
description: ExLlamaV2 is a tuned CUDA kernel set for running GPTQ and EXL2 quantized models on a single GPU. It is a favourite for consumer-grade inference because of its high tokens-per-second on small batch sizes.

name: MLX
summary: Apple Silicon ML framework with NumPy-like APIs.
description: MLX is Apple's array framework for Apple Silicon. It unifies NumPy-style ops, autograd, and compilation so you can train and run LLMs natively on M-series chips without leaving Python.

timeline.title: Open source release
timeline.description: Apple open-sources MLX for Apple Silicon.

name: KTransformers
summary: Drop-in llama.cpp with CUDA, MoE, and CPU/GPU hybrid offload.
description: KTransformers extends llama.cpp with optimized CUDA kernels for attention and MoE expert dispatch, enabling frontier-scale open models to run on a desktop with a single consumer GPU.

name: llamafile
summary: Single-file LLM executables powered by Cosmopolitan Libc.
description: llamafile packages a model and the llama.cpp runtime into one portable executable that runs on Linux, macOS, Windows, BSD, and Docker without installation.

name: SGLang
summary: Structured generation language runtime for LLMs.
description: SGLang pairs a Python DSL with a high-performance runtime (RadixAttention) to execute multi-call LLM programs with shared prefix caching and parallel tool calls.

name: LiteLLM
summary: Unified API gateway for 100+ LLM providers.
description: LiteLLM translates OpenAI-compatible calls to every major provider, adds retries, fallbacks, and cost tracking, and can be deployed as a proxy or used as a Python SDK.

name: LangGraph
summary: Graph-based orchestration for stateful, multi-agent LLM apps.
description: LangGraph is LangChain's framework for modelling agent loops as graphs with explicit state, cycles, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.

name: LlamaIndex
summary: Data framework for connecting LLMs to private documents.
description: LlamaIndex provides loaders, parsers, indexes, and query engines for ingestion pipelines. It focuses on the RAG workflow and integrates with dozens of vector stores and agent frameworks.

name: Haystack
summary: Pipeline framework for production NLP and LLM applications.
description: Haystack by deepset is a Python framework for building retrieval, RAG, and agent pipelines. Components are composable nodes connected in a directed graph.

name: AutoGen
summary: Framework for multi-agent LLM conversations.
description: AutoGen is Microsoft's framework for building applications where multiple agents converse to solve tasks. It supports human-in-the-loop patterns and code-executing agents.

name: CrewAI
summary: Role-based multi-agent orchestration framework.
description: CrewAI lets you define crews of role-playing agents with goals, tools, and a shared process. It focuses on collaborative task execution.

name: ComfyUI
summary: Node-based UI for Stable Diffusion pipelines.
description: ComfyUI is a graph-based interface where each diffusion step is a node. Power users build complex pipelines for img2img, inpainting, ControlNet, AnimateDiff, and SDXL workflows.

name: InvokeAI
summary: Professional image generation workspace built on Diffusion.
description: InvokeAI is a polished web app for Stable Diffusion with a canvas editor, model manager, and workflow builder aimed at creators.

name: Fooocus
summary: Minimalist Stable Diffusion UI inspired by Midjourney.
description: Fooocus strips Stable Diffusion down to a single screen with sensible defaults. It bundles SDXL checkpoints, inpainting, and face swap into one easy install.

name: Kohya Scripts
summary: Reference training scripts for Stable Diffusion LoRAs and checkpoints.
description: Kohya's GUI and scripts are the de-facto trainer for Stable Diffusion. They support SDXL, LoRA, DreamBooth, and full fine-tuning on consumer GPUs.

name: Axolotl
summary: YAML-driven fine-tuning framework for LLMs.
description: Axolotl wraps Hugging Face Transformers + PEFT + DeepSpeed into a single config-driven CLI. It supports LoRA, QLoRA, full fine-tuning, and dozens of open models out of the box.

name: Unsloth
summary: 2x faster LLM fine-tuning with patched kernels.
description: Unsloth rewrites attention and optimizer kernels to deliver 2x speedups and reduced memory for LoRA and full fine-tuning on a single GPU.

name: bitsandbytes
summary: 8-bit and 4-bit quantization primitives for PyTorch.
description: bitsandbytes provides LLM.int8() and 4-bit NF4 quantizers that plug into Hugging Face Transformers and PEFT for memory-efficient training and inference.

name: PEFT
summary: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning library (LoRA, IA3, adapters).
description: Hugging Face PEFT implements LoRA, prefix tuning, prompt tuning, and IA3 on top of Transformers, letting you fine-tune billion-parameter models on consumer hardware.

name: TRL
summary: Transformer Reinforcement Learning: SFT, DPO, PPO, GRPO.
description: TRL adds preference and reinforcement learning trainers on top of Transformers, including SFT, DPO, PPO, and the latest GRPO recipes.

name: Milvus
summary: Distributed vector database built for billion-scale similarity search.
description: Milvus is a cloud-native vector database written in C++ and Go. It supports IVF, HNSW, and DiskANN indexes with multi-tenant isolation and horizontal sharding.

name: Qdrant
summary: Rust-based vector search engine with rich payload filtering.
description: Qdrant is a vector database written in Rust. It pairs HNSW indexes with payload-based filtering and supports both cloud and self-hosted deployments.

name: Chroma
summary: Embedded vector database for Python-first AI apps.
description: Chroma is an open-source embedding database. It runs in-process or as a server, integrates tightly with LangChain and LlamaIndex, and ships with a built-in metadata filter.

name: Weaviate
summary: Vector database with modules for vectorization and hybrid search.
description: Weaviate is a Go-based vector database that combines dense vectors, sparse BM25, and generative re-ranking in a single GraphQL/REST API.

name: pgvector
summary: Open-source vector similarity search for PostgreSQL.
description: pgvector adds HNSW and IVF indexes to PostgreSQL so you can store embeddings next to relational data and query them with standard SQL.

name: LanceDB
summary: Embedded multimodal database built on the Lance columnar format.
description: LanceDB is an embedded vector + columnar database written in Rust. It handles vectors, metadata, and large blobs for AI applications.

name: Faiss
summary: Library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors.
description: Faiss, from Meta AI, is the C++ reference library for vector indexing. It ships IVF, HNSW, and GPU-accelerated indexes for billion-scale similarity search.

name: whisper.cpp
summary: Port of OpenAI Whisper to plain C/C++ for fast ASR.
description: whisper.cpp is the whisper inference loop rewritten in C/C++. It runs OpenAI's speech recognition models on phones, laptops, and Raspberry Pi.

name: faster-whisper
summary: CTranslate2-based Whisper implementation in Python.
description: faster-whisper runs Whisper models through the CTranslate2 inference engine, giving 4x speedup over the original Python implementation on CPU.

name: CosyVoice
summary: Open multilingual text-to-speech from FunAudioLLM.
description: CosyVoice is an open multilingual TTS model with cross-lingual voice cloning, streaming inference, and natural prosody.

name: Piper
summary: Fast local neural text-to-speech engine.
description: Piper is a fast, local neural TTS engine that runs on CPU and Raspberry Pi. It is trained on the VITS architecture and ships in dozens of voices.

name: XTTS
summary: Multilingual voice cloning TTS from Coqui.
description: XTTS is Coqui's cross-lingual voice cloning model. It generates speech in 13 languages from a six-second reference clip.

name: lm-evaluation-harness
summary: Reference framework for benchmarking LLMs.
description: EleutherAI's lm-evaluation-harness is the de-facto standard for running academic LLM benchmarks. It supports 200+ tasks out of the box and dozens of model APIs.

name: Ragas
summary: Evaluation metrics for RAG pipelines.
description: Ragas provides reference-based and reference-free metrics for retrieval-augmented generation: faithfulness, answer relevancy, context precision, and recall.

name: DeepEval
summary: Open-source LLM evaluation framework with pytest integration.
description: DeepEval is an LLM evaluation framework that plugs into pytest. It offers 14+ metrics, including G-Eval, hallucination, bias, and toxicity, and runs locally.

name: OpenCompass
summary: Open evaluation platform for large language models.
description: OpenCompass from Shanghai AI Lab is a comprehensive evaluation suite that benchmarks LLMs across 100+ datasets covering reasoning, knowledge, and code.

name: Open WebUI
summary: Self-hosted ChatGPT-style interface for local LLMs.
description: Open WebUI is a polished self-hosted chat interface for Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It supports RAG, multi-user accounts, and plugin pipelines.

name: Lobe Chat
summary: Open-source ChatGPT/LMSYS UI for individuals and teams.
description: Lobe Chat is a polished chat workspace with multi-provider support, plugin tools, and local-first deployment options. Built in Next.js.

name: Jan
summary: Local-first ChatGPT alternative that runs models on your machine.
description: Jan is an Electron-based desktop app that runs open models on your laptop with Cortex, an embedded llama.cpp runtime. It is fully offline-capable.

name: GPT4All
summary: Desktop app to run open LLMs locally with a friendly UI.
description: GPT4All is Nomic AI's cross-platform desktop client for running open LLMs locally. It ships a curated model catalog and LocalDocs for retrieval.

name: LocalAI
summary: Self-hosted OpenAI API drop-in replacement.
description: LocalAI is a REST API that mirrors the OpenAI surface and dispatches to local backends: llama.cpp, bert.cpp, whisper.cpp, and Stable Diffusion.

name: Text Embeddings Inference
summary: High-throughput inference server for text embeddings.
description: TEI is Hugging Face's Rust-based serving stack for sentence-transformer embedding models, optimized for batch throughput and low-latency vector search.
